pr.title: Split out lbheap and fix an sdata2 split
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Separated the lb heap assembly block out of asm/melee/lb/code_8000F9F8.s into a new asm/melee/lb/lbheap.s object, added that object to obj_files.mk, and corrected an sdata2 placement issue by moving lbl_804D7C80 through lbl_804D7CC0 constants into asm/melee/lb/code_80019230.s. The PR is mostly a mechanical assembly/object split with data-section hygiene, not a C decompilation change.

key_files.role: source assembly file that had the lbheap block and related data removed
key_files.evidence: 752 deleted lines, including func_800158D0 through the lbheap report/data symbols and sdata/sdata2 labels

key_files.role: new split assembly module for lbheap
key_files.evidence: 723 added lines; contains .text functions starting at func_800158D0, .data strings such as "lbheap.c", .bss lbl_80431FA0, and .sdata lbl_804D37A0
46 added lines defining lbl_804D7C80 through lbl_804D7CC0 in .section .sdata2

what_changed: Removed a contiguous lbheap-related assembly/data block from asm/melee/lb/code_8000F9F8.s.
Created asm/melee/lb/lbheap.s with macros.inc, a .text range comment for 0x8000F9F8-0x80016158, and the moved functions beginning at func_800158D0.
Moved lbheap data into the new file, including lbl_803BA380 data, strings such as "lbheap.c" and "[lbHeap] -- Report --\n", bss symbol lbl_80431FA0, and sdata symbol lbl_804D37A0.
Added asm/melee/lb/lbheap.o to obj_files.mk so the new assembly file participates in the build link order.
Fixed an sdata2 split by removing lbl_804D7C80 through lbl_804D7CC0 from code_8000F9F8.s and appending them to asm/melee/lb/code_80019230.s under .section .sdata2.
smart_moves: Used the source string "lbheap.c" as evidence for naming the new assembly module lbheap.s, giving future decompilation work a more meaningful file boundary than code_8000F9F8.s.
Kept build order explicit by adding lbheap.o immediately after code_8000F9F8.o in TEXT_O_FILES, preserving the local object sequence around the split.
Separated the lbheap module while also correcting section ownership for trailing sdata2 constants, avoiding a split that would leave constants attached to the wrong object.

The diff appears mostly mechanical: deleted assembly/data from one file and reintroduced it in the new file or corrected file, which is a low-risk way to improve module boundaries before C decompilation.
decomp_lessons: When an assembly blob contains clear embedded source-file strings such as "lbheap.c", split the blob into a named module before attempting C work.
A file split must account for all sections, not just .text. This PR moved .data, .bss, and .sdata for lbheap into lbheap.s while handling unrelated .sdata2 constants separately.

Do not assume nearby sdata2 labels belong to the newly split module. Here lbl_804D7C80-lbl_804D7CC0 were moved to code_80019230.s instead of lbheap.s, implying section boundary/order mattered more than textual proximity.
After adding a new asm file, update obj_files.mk in the correct link-order position; otherwise matching can fail even if the assembly contents are unchanged.
Large anonymous function ranges can be made more searchable and reviewable by carving out known subsystems while preserving existing labels until semantic names are available.
naming_conventions: New assembly file name lbheap.s mirrors the embedded source filename string "lbheap.c".
Existing unknown symbol names were preserved, e.g. func_800158D0, func_80015900, lbl_80431FA0, lbl_804D37A0, and lbl_804D7C80, rather than inventing semantic names without evidence.
The object path follows the project convention $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/lb/<module>.o for undecompiled lb assembly modules.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Perform assembly splits by moving entire contiguous labeled regions and their associated section data, preserving labels and instruction text.
Maintain explicit section directives in the new file: .text, .data, .bss, and .sdata are present in lbheap.s.
For small-data constants, preserve exact labels, alignment, and .4byte payloads when relocating between assembly files.
Use obj_files.mk link order as part of matching strategy; the new lbheap.o was inserted directly after code_8000F9F8.o.

The sdata2 constants lbl_804D7C80, lbl_804D7C84, lbl_804D7C88, lbl_804D7C8C, lbl_804D7C90, lbl_804D7C94, lbl_804D7C98, lbl_804D7CA0, lbl_804D7CA8, lbl_804D7CB0, lbl_804D7CB8, and lbl_804D7CC0 were preserved byte-for-byte while moved under .section .sdata2 in code_80019230.s.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review bodies were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
searchable_terms: Split out lbheap and fix an sdata2 split
follow_up_queries: Which later PR decompiled asm/melee/lb/lbheap.s into C?
What struct is represented by lbl_80431FA0 entries of size 0x1c?
Why do lbl_804D7C80-lbl_804D7CC0 belong after code_80019230.s rather than lbheap.s?
Are func_800158D0 through func_80016158 later renamed to lbHeap APIs?
Does obj_files.mk link-order placement around code_8000F9F8.o affect other lb module splits?
